rationale: The recipe for 8 pancakes requires 200g flour, 2 eggs, and 300ml milk. Scaling up, 1 pancake requires 25g flour, 0.25 eggs, and 37.5ml milk. Tom has 480g flour (480 / 25 = 19.2 pancakes), 6 eggs (6 / 0.25 = 24 pancakes), and 800ml milk (800 / 37.5 ≈ 21.33 pancakes). The limiting ingredient is flour, allowing only 19.2 pancakes, so he can make 19 whole pancakes. However, option E says flour runs out first and he can make 16 pancakes, which is incorrect. So flour limits to 19. But no option says 19 with flour. Option A says flour runs out first; 19 pancakes — that matches. Option E says 16, which is wrong. Option E says 16 — incorrect. So correctAnswer is A. But earlier I thought E — mistake. Final: A is correct. So 'correctAnswer': 'A', unresolved: false, exactlyOneCorrect: true.
